visa_free.notes: Seychelles grants visa-free entry to all ordinary passport holders except Kosovo. A Visitor's Permit is issued on arrival for up to 3 months (90 days), free of charge; extendable in 3-month increments up to 12 months total (SCR 5,000 per additional 3-month period). Visitors must demonstrate minimum funds of US$150 or equivalent per day, hold a valid return/onward ticket, and have confirmed accommodation. IMPORTANT: Although no visa is required, ALL travellers must obtain a paid Electronic Travel Authorisation (TA/ETA) prior to departure via seychelles.govtas.com or the Seychelles e-Border app. Standard processing (24 hours): EUR 10; Premium processing (6 hours): EUR 30. Failure to obtain the TA before travel may result in a penalty fee on arrival. Kosovo passport holders are explicitly refused entry as Seychelles does not recognise the State of Kosovo.

eta.notes: All travellers to Seychelles must obtain an Electronic Travel Authorisation (TA) before departure. The TA is a mandatory pre-travel requirement even though no visa is required. Applications are submitted online at seychelles.govtas.com or via the Seychelles e-Border app. Fee: EUR 10 for standard processing (24 hours); EUR 30 for premium processing (6 hours). Applications are accepted up to 10 days before travel. Documents required: valid passport, selfie, contact information, airline booking confirmation showing arrival and departure dates, accommodation booking confirmation, and payment card. Yellow fever certificate required only for travellers arriving from or transiting through yellow fever endemic countries. Kosovo passport holders are excluded from eligibility.

visa_policy.notes: Seychelles operates a universal visa-free entry policy for all nationalities except Kosovo (which is refused entry as Seychelles does not recognise the State of Kosovo). However, the Electronic Travel Authorisation (TA/ETA) is MANDATORY and PAID for all visitors before departure: EUR 10 standard (24h) / EUR 30 premium (6h). The Visitor's Permit (initial 3 months, free of charge on arrival) may be extended in 3-month increments up to a maximum of 12 months total at SCR 5,000 per period. Passport must be valid for the full duration of the intended stay.

visa_required.notes: Kosovo passport holders are refused entry. Seychelles does not recognise the State of Kosovo. ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 XKX is used as a user-assigned code for Kosovo.

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment (CBI) program exists in Seychelles. The official ICS page (ics.gov.sc/citizenship/aquisition-of-citizenship) confirms only three pathways: (1) naturalization — married to a Seychellois citizen for 15+ years with 2+ years aggregate residency and passing a citizenship exam at 80%; (2) parental/grandparental descent; (3) special circumstances (deceased Seychellois spouse). Third-party sources have referenced a USD 1M+ investment path to citizenship but this cannot be verified on any official government domain (ics.gov.sc, statehouse.gov.sc, mfa.gov.sc) and is therefore not recorded.

programs.name: Permanent Residence Permit by Investment
programs.notes: Available to investors who: (1) have made a USD 1 million or more direct investment in a Seychelles business; (2) have at least 5 years of business association from that investment; (3) have resided in Seychelles for not less than 5 years on a Dependent Permit or Gainful Occupation Permit (GOP); (4) have not been continuously absent for 1+ year without ministerial consent; (5) have not been sentenced to imprisonment exceeding 1 year. Processing fee: SCR 1,000 (non-refundable, paid upfront in cash); Permit fee if approved: SCR 350,000. Key benefits: exempt from GOP fees; may purchase residential land from government at 50% sanction fee reduction. Applications submitted in person or via prcitsection@immigration.gov.sc. Official site managed by Seychelles Investment Board (SIB) at investinseychelles@sib.gov.sc.

programs.name: Residence Permit (Family/Special Contribution)
programs.notes: Granted to persons who: (a) have a family or domestic connection with Seychelles, or (b) have made or will make a special contribution to the economic, social or cultural life of Seychelles. Does not permit gainful occupation (a separate GOP is required). Permit holder must reside in Seychelles for at least 5 days per 12-month period. Bank guarantee of SCR 20,000 required. Fee: SCR 1,000 processing fee + SCR 150,000 permit fee (5-year term, main applicant) + SCR 75,000 for spouse (if applied simultaneously) + SCR 25,000 per child endorsement. Holder is not entitled to free medical service or social security benefits.

programs.name: Gainful Occupation Permit (GOP)
programs.eligibility: Non-Seychellois who have secured employment or are self-employed in Seychelles. Employer must first obtain approval from the Ministry of Employment and Social Affairs. Application should be submitted at least one week before the employee is due to start work.
programs.notes: The GOP allows a foreign national to reside and work in Seychelles either as an employee or self-employed person. Processing fee: SCR 1,000; Permit fee: SCR 500 per month or part thereof, payable upfront for the full permit duration. Holders of a Permanent Residence Permit by Investment are exempt from GOP fees. No dedicated digital nomad, startup, talent, or remote-worker permit was found on any official government source as of June 2026.

agreements.notes: Seychelles and Lebanon signed a visa waiver agreement on November 12, 2024 covering all passport holders from both countries. The press release states 'all passport holders from the two countries will enjoy exemption from visa requirements.' Lebanese ordinary passport holders already entered Seychelles visa-free under the general policy; this agreement formalises mutual visa-free travel.

Seychelles and Egypt signed a Visa Waiver Agreement on April 5–6, 2025 covering ONLY diplomatic and official passport holders. Ordinary passport extension was discussed but NOT agreed at the time of signing. Egyptian ordinary passport holders continue to enter Seychelles under the general visa-free policy (not under this agreement).

Seychelles and the Kyrgyz Republic signed a Visa Waiver Agreement on May 4, 2026 during Minister Barry Faure's official visit. Passport types covered and effective date are not specified in the press release. Kyrgyz ordinary passport holders already entered Seychelles visa-free under the general policy.

validation_notes: CORRECTED: ETA/Travel Authorisation (TA) is NOT free. The prior record omitted the fee. MFA page and govtas.com FAQ confirm: EUR 10 for standard processing (24 hours) / EUR 30 for premium processing (6 hours). This is a mandatory paid pre-travel requirement for ALL nationalities except Kosovo.
CORRECTED: Official terminology is 'Travel Authorisation (TA)' per mfa.gov.sc, not merely 'ETA'. Both names are used across official sources; 'TA/ETA' is used in this record for clarity.
CORRECTED: Added visa_required block explicitly listing Kosovo (XKX) as the only excluded nationality, making the default_visa_free=true logic explicit and machine-readable.

CORRECTED: Added mfa.gov.sc/travelling-to-seychelles/ to source_urls in visa_policy (this page is the most detailed official entry requirements page and was missing from visa_policy.source_urls, though it appeared in sources_checked).

CORRECTED: Egypt-Seychelles agreement (April 2025) is ONLY for diplomatic/official passports — ordinary not included. The prior record correctly noted this in gaps but the agreements array now records it explicitly with passport_types=[diplomatic, official].

CORRECTED: Added agreements array with Lebanon (Nov 2024, all passports), Egypt (Apr 2025, diplomatic/official only), and Kyrgyz Republic (May 2026, passport types unspecified) — these were in sources_checked/gaps but not structured as agreement records.
CORRECTED: Added mfa.gov.sc to official_domains (was in sources_checked but missing from official_domains array).
CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: CBI program does not exist — confirmed against ics.gov.sc/citizenship/aquisition-of-citizenship.
CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: RBI programs (PRP by Investment USD 1M, Residence Permit SCR 150,000) confirmed against official sources.

CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: conditional_access remains empty — Seychelles grants unconditional ordinary-passport entry to all except Kosovo; no credential-based or passport-type-only-waiver inbound rules exist.
CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: Kosovo passport holders refused entry per ics.gov.sc/visa-and-travel/visiting-seychelles.

ADDED: outbound_official field populated from official ICS PDF 'Countries Where Seychelles Nationals Do Not Require a Visa' (ics.gov.sc/other-documents/countries-without-visa-requirements/viewdocument). Visa-on-arrival entries (Madagascar, Zimbabwe, Jordan, Oman, Qatar) separated from visa-free. India marked with ** in PDF meaning 'Visa Issued By Diplomatic Missions/Consular Post In Seychelles' — treated as e-visa/prior-arrangement required. Ethiopia, Thailand, Vietnam restricted to diplomatic/official passports only — excluded from ordinary-passport visa-free list. Belarus, Angola, Benin, Togo, Belize, French Guiana, Guyana, Martinique listed without validity details in PDF.
gaps: ETA/TA exact fee: MFA page states EUR 10.90 while govtas.com FAQ search result states EUR 10 standard / EUR 30 premium. Minor discrepancy — EUR 10.90 may reflect a payment processing fee; EUR 10 is the base fee for standard processing. Official govtas.com FAQ headings were confirmed via search results but dynamic page content could not be fully scraped.

Egypt-Seychelles agreement (April 2025): covers only diplomatic/official passports; extension to ordinary passports was 'under discussion' but no follow-up official announcement confirming extension to ordinary passports was found as of June 2026.
Kyrgyz Republic visa waiver (May 2026): passport types covered not specified in press release; full agreement text not available on official domain.
Lebanon visa waiver (November 2024): in-force date not specified; press release confirms signing but not ratification/entry-into-force date.

No official Seychelles government page found for a digital nomad, remote-worker, or workation permit; third-party aggregators reference a 'Workation Retreat Program' but no official domain confirms this.
No official confirmation of COMESA Free Movement Protocol ratification by Seychelles found on any official Seychelles government domain.
The investinseychelles.com domain is not a *.gov.sc TLD, but uses sib.gov.sc as contact email and is the official Seychelles Investment Board portal; treated as official.

Seychelles e-Israel ordinary passport visa exemption agreement (signed January 11, 2024 per mfa.gov.sc): Israeli ordinary passport holders now enter under a formal bilateral agreement, but since they already entered under the general visa-free policy, this does not change classification.
Kosovo XKX is a user-assigned ISO code, not an official ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 code; used here as widely accepted standard.

outbound_official PDF (ics.gov.sc) has no publication date. PDF appears to be from circa 2015 (latest dated entries: UAE 16 July 2015, Mozambique April 2013, Cambodia December 2012, India March 2015). The list is likely outdated and should be treated as a baseline; current access levels may differ.

sources_checked.description: Official Immigration and Civil Status Seychelles — Visiting Seychelles page; confirms visa-free for all except Kosovo; ETA required via seychelles.govtas.com
Official ICS — Visitors Permit page; confirms 90-day initial grant free of charge; SCR 5,000 per 3-month extension; up to 12 months total
Official MFA Seychelles — Travelling to Seychelles page; confirms mandatory paid TA (EUR 10.90 standard); all nationalities must apply; documents required listed
Official Seychelles Electronic Border System — mandatory TA portal for all travellers; Government of Seychelles owned; EUR 10 standard / EUR 30 premium processing fees confirmed via FAQ search
Official MFA Seychelles — Visa page; lists outbound visa requirements for Seychellois passport holders by country (NOT inbound rules for foreign nationals entering Seychelles)
Official ICS Seychelles — Seychellois Travelling Abroad page; links to PDF documents listing countries with and without visa requirements for Seychelles nationals
Official ICS Seychelles — PDF: 'Countries Where Seychelles Nationals Do Not Require a Visa'; primary source for outbound_official field; lists visa-free and visa-on-arrival countries by region
Official ICS — Residence Permit page (family/special contribution); SCR 150,000 permit fee for 5-year term
Official ICS — Gainful Occupation Permit page; SCR 500/month, 1–5 day processing
Official ICS — Acquisition of Citizenship; only naturalization, descent, and special circumstances; no CBI confirmed
Official Seychelles Investment Board — Permanent Residence Permit by Investment (USD 1M+, 5 years, SCR 350,000 fee)
Official MFA Seychelles — Egypt-Seychelles visa waiver signed April 5–6, 2025; covers ONLY diplomatic and official passport holders; ordinary under discussion
Official MFA Seychelles — Lebanon-Seychelles visa waiver signed November 12, 2024; covers all passport holders
Official MFA Seychelles — Kyrgyz Republic-Seychelles visa waiver signed May 4, 2026; passport types not specified

outbound_official.notes: Source is an official PDF published by Immigration and Civil Status (ICS), Government of Seychelles, linked from ics.gov.sc/visa-and-travel/seychellois-travelling-abroad. The PDF has no explicit publication date; the latest dated entries are UAE (16 July 2015), India (12 March 2015), Mozambique (April 2013), Cambodia (December 2012) — the list likely dates from circa 2015. Countries marked 'Issued Upon Arrival' in the PDF are placed in visa_on_arrival. India is marked ** meaning 'Visa Issued By Diplomatic Missions/Consular Post In Seychelles' — placed in e_visa as it requires prior arrangement. Ethiopia, Thailand, Vietnam listed as diplomatic/official passports only — excluded from ordinary-passport visa_free. Belarus, Angola, Benin, Togo, Belize, French Guiana, Guyana, Martinique appear in the list with no validity or condition details. UAE (3 months, as of 16 July 2015) is included as visa-free since it is in the no-visa-required document. The companion document 'Countries With Visa Requirement' was not retrieved.

e_visa.name: India
e_visa.note: Marked ** in PDF: 'Visa Issued By Diplomatic Missions/Consular Post In Seychelles' — requires visa arranged via Indian diplomatic mission in Seychelles, as of 12 March 2015
